ground_truth.title: Tutorial Books
ground_truth.main_content: This is a Goodreads shelf page displaying books shelved as 'tutorial'. The collection contains 392 books with 1-50 currently shown. The tutorial genre includes books that teach skills or serve as instructional guides across various subjects. Featured books include technical programming tutorials like 'The Rust Programming Language' by Steve Klabnik, 'Hands-On Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn and TensorFlow' by Aurélien Géron, 'Programming Perl' by Tom Christiansen, and 'The C++ Standard Library' by Nicolai M. Josuttis. Art and creative instruction is represented by 'The New Drawing on the Right Side of the Brain' by Betty Edwards, 'How to Draw: Sketch and Draw Anything, Anywhere', and 'Wild Watercolor: Weekly Lessons and Techniques for Stunning Creations'. Classical literature appears in this tutorial category including 'Dracula' by Bram Stoker, 'Frankenstein: The 1818 Text' by Mary Wollstonecraft Shelley, 'Jane Eyre' by Charlotte Brontë, and 'Wuthering Heights' by Emily Brontë. Self-help and psychology books include 'Cognitive Behavioral Therapy: Techniques for Retraining Your Brain'. The page allows users to view book ratings, add books to their reading shelves, and rate books on a 5-star scale.
with: This is a Goodreads shelf page displaying books shelved as 'tutorial'.
The Rust Programming Language by Steve Klabnik is shelved 2 times as tutorial with an average rating of 4.50.
Books shelved as tutorial include programming guides, art instruction manuals, and self-help books.

This collection shows books that teach skills or serve as instructional guides across various subjects.
